doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, Dr. Lee. I’ve been having this, like, really bad sore throat for the past three days, and it’s just getting worse. I also started feeling feverish yesterday, and I’ve had some chills, but I haven’t measured my temperature or anything. It hurts a lot when I swallow, especially solids… it’s kinda hard to eat.

doctor_turn: When did the sore throat first start?
patient_turn: Uh, it started about three days ago, I think. It was, um, pretty constant, but it’s just been getting worse since then, you know?

doctor_turn: Can you describe where the pain is located in your throat?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, it’s mostly in the back of my throat, I guess? It really hurts when I swallow, and sometimes even when I drink cold liquids. It’s just, like, really uncomfortable all the time.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Um, I’d say it’s more of a dull pain, but it can get pretty sharp when I try to swallow. It’s just, like, really irritating and makes it hard to eat or drink anything. Honestly, it’s been pretty exhausting.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the sore throat, and has it changed since then?
Where exactly do you feel the pain in your throat?
patient_turn: Oh, um, it’s mostly in the back of my throat, like, where the tonsils are, I guess? It’s just really sore back there, and it feels worse when I try to swallow anything.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to any other areas, like your ears or neck?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, I’ve noticed that my neck feels kinda sore too, like, I can feel some lumps there, which I think are swollen lymph nodes. It doesn’t really hurt in my ears, but I guess the pain in my throat makes everything feel a bit more uncomfortable overall.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or more like pressure?
patient_turn: Oh, I just remembered I need to get my car washed later.

doctor_turn: What makes the pain better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Well, um, it definitely gets worse when I swallow, especially solids. I’ve tried using lozenges, and they help a little, but it’s only temporary relief, you know? Cold liquids can sometimes help, but not always. It’s just really frustrating.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like—sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Um, I’d say it’s mostly a dull pain, but it can get sharp when I swallow. It’s just really uncomfortable and makes everything feel harder, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any other symptoms, like breathlessness, dizziness, or nausea?
Have you checked your temperature at home recently?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I haven’t actually measured my temperature. I just feel, um, feverish and have had some chills, but I don’t have a thermometer to check it. I’m kinda worried it might be something serious.

doctor_turn: Is it hard for you to eat because of the pain in your throat?
patient_turn: Yeah, definitely. Eating is really difficult because of the pain when I swallow. I mean, I try to eat, but it just hurts so much, especially with solids. I’ve been, um, managing to eat a little, but it’s not easy at all.

doctor_turn: Have you traveled anywhere recently or been in contact with anyone who was sick?
patient_turn: Um, no, I haven’t traveled anywhere recently, and I don’t know of anyone around me who’s been sick. It’s just, like, really strange that I’m feeling this way.

doctor_turn: How are you currently managing your Crohn's disease?
patient_turn: Oh, um, I’m managing it with sulfasalazine, which I’ve been on since I was diagnosed last year. I haven’t had any major issues lately, but, you know, I’m just a bit worried about how this sore throat might affect everything.

doctor_turn: Do you have any allergies, like to pollen, dust, or certain foods?
patient_turn: Uh, no, I don’t have any allergies to environmental factors or foods that I know of. I mean, I haven’t had any issues with that, so… I’m not really sure if that’s relevant.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have a history of throat issues or similar health problems?
patient_turn: Um, not that I know of, but my family does have a history of Crohn’s disease and ulcerative colitis. I think my brother has asthma and eczema too, but, like, nothing specifically about throat issues.

doctor_turn: Have you had any surgeries or are you taking any other medications besides sulfasalazine?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any surgeries aside from a colonoscopy last year, and, um, sulfasalazine is the only medication I’m taking right now. I haven’t had any other significant health issues, so it’s just that.
